title: Repo inspiration / attribution manifest
policy: Design patterns are adapted from inspected swarm repos. No third-party code is vendored or committed. The repos were shallow-cloned into the gitignored sandbox 21_repos/third_party_runtime_sandbox/ for read-only LICENSE/README inspection only - not installed, not executed, not committed.

rust_toolchain.note: No Cargo.toml in any inspected repo. The Ghoti launcher is pure Python standard library; no Rust is introduced.

patterns_adapted: explicit per-task dependencies that drive parallel waves
the orchestrator that plans the work also coordinates execution (keeps context)
give each worker exactly which files it owns
repos.status: patterns only; license unverified -> no code reuse, no install, no execution

patterns_adapted: one-command goal -> an orchestrated agent team
named roles and CLI-agent-agnostic membership (Claude / Codex as members)
repos.status: patterns only; not installed, not executed

patterns_adapted: decompose a task into a dependency graph
file conflict detection so agents do not step on each other (overlap detection)
budget enforcement to stop runaway cost
a quality gate that reviews outputs (auditor role)
status + replay shaped for visualization (Agent Arena)
repos.status: patterns only; not installed, not executed

patterns_adapted: security-scanner patterns (Ghoti already has equivalents in public_repo_security_audit.py)
repos.status: guidance only; ECC = Everything Claude Code; not installed, not executed

attribution_note: Ghoti's dry-run launcher is original code that re-expresses the public design patterns listed above. For the MIT-licensed repos, any future small permitted snippet reuse would carry the copyright notice; no snippet is reused in this milestone.
